properties.remark: Golf ball sized hail reported in the Wolf Creek Community.
A lot of pea sized hail, also.
Report from mPING: Half Dollar (1.25 in.).
Semi blown over on Highway 85 just north of town.
Broken hailstone of 3.6 inch measured. Corrected for size, time, and more precise location.
Several 3.1 inch diameter hail stones. Corrected for size.
Two semis were overturned on I-90 at mile markers 114 & 119.
Winds to at least 60 mph. Windows broken in the house and camper.
Took out Jackson Co. Deputy Sheriff's windshield on I-90

*** 2 Inj *** Tornado touched down approx 5 miles south of the town of Kadoka in the White River Valley. The tornado travelled 3 miles in the valley and was a 1/4 mile wide at its largest. It destroyed 2 homes and damaged a 3rd, along with damaging numerous trees in the river valley. Damage survey was conducted the following morning (6/29/25)
*** 2 Inj *** Tornado Start Time 6:45 PM MDT at 5 miles SSW Kadoka/ End Time 7:00 PM MDT 6 miles SSE Kadoka. Rating: EF-2
Photo shows hail from around half dollar to ping pong ball sized. Fell from 530 AM to 540 AM.
Largest hail about the size of a quarter. Most hail was smaller, pea to nickel
Photos show several stones of quarter to at least half dollar size
Nickel to quarter size hail. Ongoing during call around 527 pm.
Estimated dime up to quarter hail.
Photos show multiple large trees uprooted
Water over Hwy 116 swept a car off the road. No injuries to the occupants. Flooding was short-lived. EM suspects a nearby stock dam gave way.
Semi blown over on I-90 near MM146.
Most of the hail was quarter size, some pieces were about 1.5 inch in diameter.
Most of the hail was quarter size, some pieces were about 1.5 inch in diameter.
Most of the hail was smaller, larger pieces up to 1 inch in diameter.
Survey team determined that an EF-1 landspout tornado impacted a home and adjcent homestead 10 NW of Moorcroft.
A few of the larger pieces were 1 inch in diameter, most was smaller.
Most quarter size. Been hailing for 15 minutes and still hailing.
Updated location. 8 SSE of Tuthill was the incorrect location.

Very gusty winds from all directions, but less than 60 mph. Very heavy rainfall.
